“如果有人说他们在这个行业有预见力，并说他们知道未来会在哪里，我只是质疑这样做是否明智，”他在一次会议上表示，该会议探讨风投公司甚至在存在之前就如何寻找新的市场。“因为如果你能想出来，你就能想出主意，你有足够的能力把所有的东西拼凑在一起，为什么你不能找到这个企业呢？”
相反，押注未来的关键是学会问那些愚蠢的问题。Kanji说：“我认为，在风投行业里，不做聪明人，把自己训练成愚蠢的人，然后问一些愚蠢的问题，实际上是完全没有问题的。”“我想很多人可能太害羞了，不敢这么做。而且很多人可能太厌恶风险了，当他们并不真正了解他们投资的是什么时，他们就会开支票。但这些东西中的很多都是闪闪发光的时刻。
其中一个引人注目的时刻是霍克斯顿风险投资公司(Hoxton Ventures)对Deliveroo的投资，Deliveroo是一家外卖送餐服务公司，与UberEats竞争，帮助几乎每一家餐厅都变成了送餐服务。然而，汉字提醒我们，这家欧洲独角兽公司并不是第一家尝试外卖送货的公司，但新技术，即廉价的智能手机加上GPS和路由算法，意味着现在是时候了。
“人们确实尝试过送货，”他说，“他们早在90年代就尝试过。每个人都会忘记这一点。纽约市有一家叫Cosmo的公司，它会按需给你买一品脱冰激凌。你知道，因为他们用的是寻呼机，所以从来没用过。比如，你还记得寻呼机吗？他们就是这样管理舰队的。他们不能移动舰队。他们不能用任何有效的方式把司机送到公寓，把司机送到商店。…。对于整个行业来说，送货方面的突破是你有了智能手机，你可以给司机提供智能手机，你可以跟踪司机在做什么，这很好，因为这样你就可以用智能手机…进行物流路由。电灯泡时刻“。
Kanji说，尽管它们是非常不同的业务和市场，但霍克斯顿的另外两个独角兽，巴比伦和黑暗踪迹，涉及类似的灯泡时刻。然而，直到有人走进门向你解释，你才会明白那一刻。“那么你的自然问题就是…。为什么现在选择…。到底有什么改变了？比如，是什么让这件事如此有趣？为什么一年前没有人想出这个？这类事情几乎总是有原因的。然后这项工作更难的部分是…。你真的要选第一名吗？“。
进入或帮助创造新市场往往不是没有争议-巴比伦和Deliveroo都出于不同的原因吸引了争议。由于真正的颠覆不可避免地会产生社会后果，这位霍克斯顿的联合创始人认为，它经常会引发伦理问题，而这些问题并不总是能够在早期就预料到。然而，随着情况变得更加明朗，他表示，风投们应该绝对关心，当然还有创始人和首席执行官。
“科技行业不断受到的批评之一是，我认为我们行业…的成熟度。我们的行为更像青少年。成为自由意志主义者是很棒的，成为自由市场并说市场会解决问题是很棒的。但你会和社会上很多其他地方有接触。你必须弄清楚，而且我认为，在…方面取得领先。影响会是什么，并且更负责任。“
note_en:
“If anyone says that they have predictive power in this industry and says they know where the future is gonna be, I just question the wisdom of this,” he said during a session exploring how VCs seek out new markets before they even exist. “Because if you could figure it out, you could come up with the idea, you’re capable enough to be able to put all the pieces together, why would you not found the business?”
 Instead, the key to betting on the future is to learn to ask the stupid questions. “I think it’s actually perfectly fine in the venture industry to not be the smart person and to kind of train yourself to be stupid and ask the stupid questions,” said Kanji. “I think a lot of people are probably too shy to do that. And a lot of people [are] probably too risk averse to then write the check when they don’t really understand exactly what it is that they’re investing into. But a lot of this stuff is a lightbulb moment”.
  One of those lightbulb moments was  Hoxton Ventures’   investment in Deliveroo, the takeout food delivery service that competes with UberEats and helped turn almost every restaurant into a food delivery service. However, Kanji reminded us that the European unicorn wasn’t the first company to try takeout delivery, but new technology, in the form of cheap smartphones coupled with GPS and routing algorithms, meant the timing was now right.
 “People did try delivery,” he said, “they tried it back in the 90s. Everyone forgets about that. There’s a company in New York City called Cosmo that would go off and like get you a pint of ice cream on demand. You know, it never worked because they used pagers. Like, do you remember pagers? Like, that’s how they ran the fleet. They couldn’t move the fleet around. They couldn’t get the driver to the apartment and the driver to the store in any kind of efficient way… The breakthrough for delivery, and for that whole industry, was you had smartphones, you could give smartphones to the drivers, you could track what the driver was doing, which is good because then you could route logistics, you know, with a smartphone… light bulb moment”.
 Kanji said that, although they are very different businesses and markets, Hoxton’s two other unicorns, Babylon and Darktrace, involved similar lightbulb moments. Yet you don’t get that light bulb moment until someone walks in the door and explains it to you. “Then your natural question is… why now… what’s actually changed? Like, what makes this so interesting? Why didn’t someone come up with this a year ago? There’s almost always usually a reason for that kind of stuff. And then then the harder part of the job is … are you really picking number one?”
 Entering or helping to create new markets is often not without controversy — which both  Babylon and  Deliveroo has attracted for different reasons. As real disruption inevitably creates societal consequences, it often raises ethical questions that, the Hoxton co-founder argues, aren’t always possible to anticipate early on. However, as the picture becomes clearer, he says VCs should absolutely care, along with, of course, founders and CEOs.
 “One of the constant criticisms in the tech industry is, I think the maturity of our industry… we behave more like teenagers. And it’s great to be libertarian, it’s great to be free markets and say markets are gonna sort it out. But you’re gonna have touch points with a lot of other places in society. You’ve got to figure out, and I think, get ahead in terms of…what the impact is going to be, and be more responsible”.
title: Hoxton Ventures的侯赛因·坎吉(Hussein Kanji)表示，风险投资公司必须训练自己“问愚蠢的问题”
title_en: VCs have to train themselves to ‘ask the stupid questions’, says Hoxton Ventures’ Hussein Kanji

在过去的15年里，我在这个博客上写了很多关于这两个大型移动操作系统对软件分发造成的重大负面影响的文章。我强烈反对苹果和谷歌对在iOS和Android上运行的移动应用程序的垄断。
我支持史诗/堡垒之夜与苹果就苹果向开发者收取在其应用商店分发的30%税一事展开斗争。但除了税收之外，这些垄断企业让我感到不安的是，它们对广泛的科技行业征收的创新税，以及它们的服务条款/规则。
没有比Crypto更好的地方看到这一点，Crypto是计算领域的下一波浪潮(仅次于网络和移动)。去中心化密码应用程序(Dapps)没有成为主流的原因有很多，但其中之一肯定是苹果和谷歌的应用程序商店不允许去中心化应用程序所需的一些重要功能。

最近，苹果宣布了一种让开发者建议更新App Store策略的方法。我们的团队正计划提交一份正式请求，要求苹果允许其用户赚取密码，并查看分散的金融应用程序列表。我们会随时向您通报我们得到的最新消息。
-布莱恩·阿姆斯特朗(@Brian_Armstrong)2020年9月11日。
Coinbase、Etic和Spotify并不是唯一与苹果和谷歌抗争的公司。他们只是足够大，足够受到保护，可以公开他们的挣扎。事实是，每一个通过这两个应用商店分发软件的开发人员都在与它们作斗争。
在什么样的世界里，让两家实力雄厚的大公司完全控制手机上的软件分发又有什么意义呢？这在任何世界都是没有意义的。它必须停止。
note_en:
I have written extensively on this blog over the last decade and a half about the significant negative consequences that the two large mobile operating systems have on distribution of software. I am strongly opposed to the monopolies that Apple and Google have over mobile apps that run on iOS and Android.
 I am rooting for Epic/Fortnite in their battle with Apple over the 30% tax that Apple charges developers for distribution in their app store. But more than the tax, what bothers me about these monopolies is the innovation tax they impose on the broad tech sector with their terms of service/rules.
 There is no better place to see that than crypto, the next big wave in computing (after web and mobile). There are a number of reasons that decentralized crypto apps (dapps) have not gone mainstream, but certainly one of them is that the Apple and Google app stores don’t allow a number of important features that decentralized apps require.
 The founder and CEO of our portfolio company Coinbase, Brian Armstrong, explained this well in a  tweetstorm last week:
     Recently Apple announced a way for developers to suggest updates to the App Store policies. Our team is planning to submit a formal request for Apple to allow its users to earn crypto and see a list of decentralized finance apps. We'll keep you updated on what we hear back.
— Brian Armstrong (@brian_armstrong)  September 11, 2020
 Coinbase, Epic, and Spotify are not alone in their struggles with Apple and Google. They are simply large enough and protected enough to go public with their struggles. The truth is every developer that distributes software through these two app stores struggles with them.
 In what world does it makes sense for two large and powerful companies to completely control software distribution on mobile phones? In no world does it make sense. It must stop.
title: 对于像苹果和谷歌这样强大的两家大公司来说，完全控制手机上的软件分发是没有意义的，它必须停止
title_en: It makes no sense for two large and powerful companies like Apple and Google to completely control software distribution on mobile phones and it must stop

note:
真正的海盗湾网站是从ThePirateBay.org运营的，但该网站总是有许多备份域名，以防万一。
这包括Piratebay.org(没有)和ThePiratebay.com。在前业主似乎忘记延长登记后，前者在本周早些时候的拍卖会上以5万美元的价格售出。.com域名也失效了，将于本周末拍卖。
高昂的价格对于海盗湾域名来说是相当不寻常的。许多域名经纪人购买这些类型的域名是为了通过广告馈送将其货币化，但在这个价格下，这真的不是一笔划算的交易。
这让我们不禁想知道买主到底想干什么。他或她是否计划开始复制海盗湾，希望接管Torrent网站的部分流量？它会不会成为恶意软件投资的“海盗陷阱”，甚至是一个蜜罐？
几个小时前，计划变得更加清晰，但这并不是任何人所期待的。今天访问Piratebay.org的人将看到“海盗湾影业”与“我感觉幸运的制片厂”合作的一条消息，宣布他们的电影项目“激流人”(The Torrent Man)。
他们宣布：“是的，这是官方的，海盗湾影业正在开始拍摄”激流人“，这是一部独立的故事片，讲述了黑暗网络、激流世界、密码和他们背后的真实人物。”
虽然这听起来很有趣，但到目前为止，这些有抱负的电影制作人似乎没有太多其他的东西。在花费5万美元购买Piratebay.org域名后，他们现在希望以近200万美元的价格出售该域名，为他们的项目筹集资金。
此外，他们还需要一个电影剧本，以及几乎整个摄制组，他们希望将其众包。
“我们需要演员、剧组成员和作者(！)。因为我们正在众包电影剧本。有没有你能想到的不寻常的转折，有没有你想看的角色，有没有疯狂的故事情节？“。
至少可以说，这一切都非常不寻常。根据公开提供的信息，网站背后的人似乎除了标题之外，基本上什么都没有。虽然这可能是一种“艺术化的点对点”的内容创建方式，但谨慎是有必要的。
与我们交谈过的领域专家说，这个域名要价200万美元根本不现实。这是有道理的，因为之前感兴趣的经纪人已经在本周的拍卖中出价，当价格过高时就退出了。
这就是说，“激流人”的想法将在Piratebay.org域名上获得大量关注，这使得它随着反向链接数量的增加而变得更有价值。我们并不是说这是真正的目标，但在没有更多信息的情况下，我们有点怀疑。
也许我们太谨慎了，但在没有更多信息的情况下，整个计划似乎相当混乱。例如，该网站提到“最初的拍摄因Covid而推迟”，这很奇怪，因为当时还没有剧本或剧组。
我们已经联系了“海盗湾影业”了解更多细节，并期待听到更多。当他们的答案出来时，我们会更新这篇文章。
note_en:
The real Pirate Bay website operates from ThePiratebay.org, but the site always had many backup domains in place, just in case.
 This included Piratebay.org (without  the) as well as ThePiratebay.com. The former was  sold for $50,000 at auction earlier this week after the previous owner seemingly forgot to extend the registration. The .com domain also lapsed and will be auctioned off this weekend.
 The high price tag is quite unusual for a Pirate Bay domain. Many domain brokers buy these types of domains to monetize them through advertising feeds, but at this price, it isn’t really a good deal.
 This made us wonder what the buyer was up to. Could he or she be planning to start a Pirate Bay copy, hoping to take over part of the torrent site’s traffic? Could it become a malware invested ‘pirate trap’, or even a honeypot?
  A few hours ago the plan became more became clear but it’s not what anyone expected. People who visit  Piratebay.org today will see a message from “PirateBay Pictures,” in collaboration with “I’m Feeling Lucky Studios,” announcing their film project; The Torrent Man.
 “Yes, it’s official, the PirateBay Pictures is starting to film The Torrent Man, an indie feature movie about the darknet, torrenting world, crypto, and the real people behind them,” they announce.
  While that sounds intriguing, it appears that thus far, the aspiring filmmakers don’t have much else. After spending $50,000 on the Piratebay.org domain they now hope to sell the domain for nearly $2 million to raise funds for their project.
  In addition, they also need a film script, and pretty much the entire film crew, which they hope to crowdsource.
 “We need actors, crew members, and authors (!) as we are crowdsourcing the movie script. Any unusual twists you can think of, any characters you want to see, crazy storylines?”
 This is all highly unusual, to say the least. Based on the information that’s presented in public it seems that the people behind the site basically have nothing, except for a title. While that could be an ‘artsy peer-to-peer’ way of creating content, caution is warranted.
  The domain experts we’ve spoken with say that asking $2 million for the domain isn’t realistic at all. That makes sense, as the brokers who were previously interested already bid in this week’s auction and bailed out when the price went too high.
 That said, ‘The Torrent Man’ idea will get plenty of attention for the Piratebay.org domain, which makes it more valuable with an increased number of backlinks. We’re not saying that this is the true goal but without more information, we’re a bit skeptical.
 Perhaps we’re being too cautious, but without more information, the entire plan seems quite confusing. For example, the site mentions that the “original shoot got pushed back due to Covid,” which is odd as there was no script or crew yet.
 We have reached out to “PirateBay Pictures” for more details and are looking forward to hearing more. We will update this article when their answers come in.
title: 盗版网站(Piratebay.org)现在被用来众包“激流人”(The Torrent Man)电影
title_en: Piratebay.org Now Being Used to Crowdsource “The Torrent Man” Film
